rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM C. MARTIN VS. THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, April 9, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporation's motion for summary judgment, or in the alternative, summary adjudication: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE LILLIE VS. CERTAINTEED CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, April 9, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Verizon California, Inc.'s motion to dismiss pursuant to C.C.P. 583.410 is off calendar at the moving party's request. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH PALLADINO VS. KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC et al(preliminary fact sheet)
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File A Complaint-In-Intervention Pursuant To Ccp Section 387; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, April 9, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Proposed intervenors Hartford Fire Insurance Company, et al.'s (on behalf of their insured defendant Rich-Tex, Inc.) motion for leave to file a complaint-in-intervention pursuant to C.C.P. 387 is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order conforming to CRC 3.1362 and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order conforming to CRC 3.1362 and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
